violations.claim: REACHABLE: containsReturn true for conditional return inside guard consequence
violations.smt2:
; REACHABLE: containsReturn true for conditional return inside guard consequence
; Guard does NOT always return — log CAN execute when guard is true
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 256
(declare-const guard_taken Int)            ; 1 = guard condition evaluated true
(declare-const inner_cond_true Int)        ; 1 = nested condition inside guard body is true
(declare-const outer_fn_returned Int)      ; 1 = outer function returned before log
(declare-const log_executed Int)           ; 1 = log statement executes
(declare-const containsReturn_result Int)  ; 1 = containsReturn(consequence) = true
(declare-const neg_guard_in_conditions Int); 1 = !(guard) pushed into path conditions

; Code transition: guard body has an inner if with a return inside
; containsReturn recurses into all namedChildren and finds return_statement
(assert (= containsReturn_result 1))

; Code transition: containsReturn=true => condition negated and added
(assert (=> (= containsReturn_result 1) (= neg_guard_in_conditions 1)))

; Execution trace: guard=true, inner condition=false => no return => log executes
(assert (= guard_taken 1))
(assert (= inner_cond_true 0))
; when guard=true but inner condition=false, the return is not reached
(assert (=> (and (= guard_taken 1) (= inner_cond_true 0)) (= outer_fn_returned 0)))
(assert (=> (= outer_fn_returned 0) (= log_executed 1)))

; Violation: log executes with guard=true, but path conditions claim !(guard)
(assert (= guard_taken 1))
(assert (= log_executed 1))
(assert (= neg_guard_in_conditions 1))
(check-sat)
